data.title: Japan 10-Year Yield Hits Over 16-Year High
data.description:
Japan’s 10-year government bond yield surged toward 1.6% on Monday, reaching its highest levels in over 16 years as investors piled on bets that the Bank of Japan will continue raising interest rates this year to normalize monetary settings.

Last week, BOJ Deputy Governor Shinichi Uchida signaled that the central bank may hike rates further if economic forecasts align with expectations, emphasizing that Japan’s exit from ultra-loose monetary policy is only beginning.

Uchida also highlighted that monetary conditions remain highly accommodative, with the BOJ’s reduction in JGB holdings still limited.

Meanwhile, recent data showed that Japan posted a current account deficit in January for the first time in two years, as imports outpaced exports.

Additionally, inflation-adjusted real wages—a key measure of consumer purchasing power—fell 1.8% year-on-year, marking the first decline in three months.

data.title: Sappe's Business Recovery in 2025 Faces Some Headwinds — Market Talk
data.description: Sappe's business recovery in 2025 faces some headwinds, Thanachart Securities' Pattadol Bunnak says in a research report, as the brokerage downgrades the stock to hold from buy and cuts its target price to THB41.00 from THB87.00. Some issues in four export markets comprising 35% of the beverage producer's total sales have emerged, the analyst says. The company expects no orders from its U.K. distributor in 1H partly owing to ongoing inventory destocking, while it has problems with its Indonesian distributor, and its competition in South Korea is re-emerging, the analyst notes. The brokerage cuts its earnings estimates for Sappe by 19% for 2025, 28% for 2026, and 35% for 2027. Shares are 3.3% lower at THB37.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: United Laboratories International Unit's Antibiotic Passes Quality, Efficacy Consistency Evaluation
data.description:
United Laboratories International said Ceftriaxone Sodium for Injection has received approval from China's National Medical Products Administration as having passed the consistency evaluation of quality and efficacy for generic drugs, a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The drug was submitted by the Zhongshan Branch of Zhuhai United Laboratories, a unit of the intermediate and finished products and bulk medicine company.

The drug is an antibiotic for the treatment of infections caused by sensitive pathogens, including respiratory tract, abdominal, renal and urinary tract, reproductive system, and preoperative infections.

data.title: Finnish Industrial Output Slowest in 8 Months
data.description:
Industrial production in Finland rose by 0.5% year-on-year in January 2025, following a downwardly revised 1.5% increase in the previous month.

This marked the eighth consecutive month of growth in industrial activity but the softest in the sequence, as output significantly slowed for mining and quarrying (3.6% vs 13.2% in December).

At the same time, production declined for electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply (-7.9% vs 3.6%).

Meanwhile, output increased further for manufacturing (1.9% vs 1.3%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial activity advanced by 1.1% in January, rebounding from an upwardly revised 1.6% drop in the preceding period.

data.title: Commodity stocks help Australian shares eke out gains
data.description:
Key points:
  • ASX 200 hovers below 8,000 level
  • Benchmark snaps 4-day losing streak
  • Gold, energy stocks climb over 1% each

Australian shares closed slightly higher on Monday after a four-day losing streak, as gold and mining stocks rose on strong underlying commodity prices.

The S&P/ASX 200 index rose 0.2% to 7,962.3, hovering near a six-month low touched on Friday. It has declined nearly 7% from an all-time high scaled on February 14.

Uncertainties regarding global trade and growth have risen as the United States has imposed fresh tariffs on its trading partners including China, leading to a sharp drop in Australian shares last week.

If the market falls further, "we could see the index back to 7,800 before it stabilises and looks attractive. That would be strong support," said Henry Jennings, senior analyst and portfolio manager at Marcustoday Financial Newsletter.

"Banks are not yet at attractive levels and resources remain a bull trap whilst China is continuing to struggle economically," Jennings said.

Latest data from China pointed to further deflationary pressures, although it raised hopes for additional supportive measures from the country to meet its annual growth target. This helped iron ore futures eke out gains.

Australian mining stocks climbed 0.9%. BHP Group and Rio Tinto jumped 0.6% and 3.1%, respectively.

Gold stocks rose 1.4% after bullion prices gained on a weaker dollar and safe-haven flows, while energy stocks climbed 1.5%.

Financials inched 0.1% lower, with Commonwealth Bank of Australia and ANZ Group down 0.2% and 0.6%, respectively.

Financials fell more than 4% last week as their rich valuations made them the prime targets during the market sell-off.

There might be some downside for the big four banks for the time being after Friday's sell-off, said Junvum Kim, Asia Pacific senior sales trader at Saxo Markets.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index advanced 0.9% to 12,515.87.

Shares of Fonterra rose 3.6% after the dairy giant forecast higher annual earnings due to robust volume and margin growth at its consumer operations.

data.title: EU Commission Flags Potential Consumer Rights Violations in Second-Hand Online Market
data.description:
The European Commission and national consumer protection authorities said an investigation determined that nearly half of second-hand online traders fail to properly inform consumers of their return rights.

The so-called sweep investigation, conducted across 25 European Union member states, as well as Iceland and Norway, assessed 356 online traders selling second-hand goods, including clothing, electronics, and toys. Authorities identified 185 traders as potentially violating EU consumer law, according to a March 7 release.

Findings showed that 40% of traders did not clearly inform consumers of their right to return products within 14 days without justification, while 45% failed to correctly communicate return rights for faulty goods. Meanwhile, 57% did not respect the minimum one-year legal guarantee for second-hand goods.

Consumer authorities will now determine whether to take action against non-compliant traders.

data.title: Pennon Group Raised to Buy From Neutral by Citigroup
Babcock International Target Raised to 885p From 575p by Berenberg
Babcock International Raised to Buy From Hold by Berenberg
Bernstein Raises Lufthansa's Price Target on Earnings Growth Prospects
data.description:
Bernstein on March 7 reiterated Deutsche Lufthansa's outperform rating and increased the price target to 9 euros from 8 euros, anticipating strong demand and growth in earnings.

After delivering "strong" passenger airline results in the 2024 fourth quarter, the German flag carrier said the outlook for Easter and summer 2025 is "very encouraging," particularly on the Transatlantic and Intra-Europe. Meanwhile, disciplined supply, dollar strength, and Lufthansa's long-haul capacity exposure will ensure that the North Atlantic remains its best market, according to the research firm.

"2025 is looking up for Lufthansa," the analyst said. "The group has soft comps as it laps strikes in H1, and ongoing yield support from high exposure to the strong North American market. Management sees "significant" EBIT improvement this year, which we expect to continue into 2026 as work continues to fix the group's eponymous airline."

Huanghe Enterprise to Buy Back Shares of Up to 30 Million Yuan; Shares Up 10%
data.description:
Lanzhou Huanghe Enterprise plans to repurchase shares worth between 20 million yuan and 30 million yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on March 7.

The Chinese beverage company will buy back from 2.1 million shares to 3.1 million shares for up to 9.70 yuan per share within 12 months after shareholders' approval.

The repurchased shares will be used for equity incentives or employee stock ownership plans, the disclosure said.

The company's shares soared 10% in recent trade.

Guotong Pipeline Bags Mixing Tower Project for 100 Million Yuan; Shares Rise 7%
data.description:
Xinjiang Guotong Pipeline won Jiangyin Yuanjing Investment's mixing tower project for 99.9 million yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Saturday.

The Chinese concrete pipe manufacturer will deliver 45 concrete wind turbine towers within five months starting April, the filing said.

The company's shares jumped less than 7% in recent trade.

data.title: Australian Shares Flat Amidst Rising US Economic Concerns; Cobre Enters Earn-In Agreement With BHP Unit for Botswana Projects
data.description:
Australian shares were flat with a positive bias on Monday's close even as investors' confidence in continued US economic outperformance waned.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index was little changed to close at 7,962.3.

Tariffs on China, Mexico, and Canada, rising unemployment, and cuts to

the federal workforce are fueling concerns about a potential slowdown in the US, Bloomberg reported.

"It's getting harder to make out the shape of the economy through the fog of Trump 2.0's firings and tariffs," said Yardeni Research's President Ed Yardeni as quoted in the report.

"No wonder the stock market's default position is risk-off and stocks have been correcting," Yardeni added.

In company news, Cobre signed an earn-in agreement with a wholly-owned unit of BHP Group for its Kitlanya East and Kitlanya West copper projects in Botswana. Shares fell past 3% on close.

Johns Lyng Group will be removed from the S&P/ASX 200 Index prior to market opening on March 24. Shares of the group fell 12% at market close.

Lastly, Star Entertainment Group confirmed it received an unsolicited, non-binding proposal from the New York Stock Exchange-listed Bally's.

In a separate filing, the cash-strapped casino operator also disclosed various liquidity initiatives amid efforts to stay afloat, including a refinancing proposal that could generate up to AU$940 million and a senior secured AU$250 million bridge facility with funds managed by King Street Capital Management.

data.title: Oman & Emirates Investment Postpones Board Meeting for Results Approval
data.description:
Oman & Emirates Investment Holding Co. (ADX:OEIHC) said Monday that its board meeting to approve the financial statements for the period ended Dec. 31, 2024, was rescheduled to Tuesday.

Directors of the Oman-based investment company were originally scheduled to meet on Monday. The trading of the company's shares is suspended until the results of the meeting are disclosed.

data.title: Aavas Financiers shares surge 9% amid high trading volumes
data.description:
The shares of Aavas Financiers soared nearly 9 percent to hit an intraday high of Rs 1,845 apiece in morning trade on March 10. The stock was one of the top gainers on the BSE Smallcap index. This comes as the stock saw strong buying demand from investors.

Nearly 1.78 lakh shares of the company were involved in active trade, as seen at 10.40 am. This is over 12 times higher than the stock's 10-day average trading volume.

The Jaipur-based housing finance company has seen a strong uptick in its share price in the past months, and is now hovering near its 52-week high of Rs 1,979 apiece, which it had hit on June 19 last year.

Earlier in February, domestic brokerage Prabhudas Lilladher had retained its 'Accumulate' rating on the stock, with a target price of Rs 1,900 per share. Notably, this is nearly 3 percent higher than the stock's current market price (CMP).

Earlier in August 2024, Aquilo House Pte Ltd and CVC Capital Partners had announced an open offer to acquire up to 26 percent of Aavas Financiers Limited's expanded voting share capital at Rs 1,766.69 per fully paid up equity share.

The open offer was made for acquisition of over 2 crore equity shares of face value of Rs 10 each worth up to Rs 3,664 crore. JM Financial was appointed as the manager to the open offer.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before making any investment decisions.

data.title: China Aims to Maintain Stable Employment in 2025
data.description:
China’s employment is expected to remain stable this year despite the challenge of creating more jobs, Minister of Human Resources and Social Security Wang Xiaoping said Sunday during an annual parliamentary session.

Wang emphasized that the government will expand job opportunities in key sectors while increasing resources and funding to support employment, with a target of creating over 12 million new urban jobs.

Efforts will focus on advanced manufacturing, emerging consumer trends, new industries, and major engineering projects, while also supporting employment in rural and urban grassroots areas and small and medium-sized enterprises.

Additionally, the government will introduce more policies to assist college graduates and young job seekers, stabilize social recruitment by expanding grassroots hiring, and implement a million-job internship program.
